Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂). These findings suggest significant renal impairment, likely acute or chronic kidney disease, with possible tubular dysfunction. The urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) may indicate underlying infection or hematuria, though leukocyte esterase and nitrites are negative. Very low HbA1c is unusual and may reflect anemia or lab error. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation and further clinical correlation.
